#+title: Shortcode Test Page
#+description: Tests all shortcode types and org blocks
#+date: [2026-03-27 Fri]
:PROPERTIES:
:tags: test, widgets
:author: Xavier Brinon
:END:

This page tests that Liquid shortcodes survive the org-to-Markdown
conversion pipeline and that org blocks map to their Markdown
equivalents.

* Simple shortcodes

{% alert 'It seems to be the beginning of a great documentation story!', 'success', 'Howdy!' %}

{% iconCard 'Configure', 'Start entering your own settings into `settings.json` at the root of the project.', 'faders' %}

{% iconCard 'Write', 'Easily write documentation with Markdown and HTML.', 'pen' %}

* Paired shortcodes

#+begin_export html
{% sandbox %}
<div class="test-sandbox">
  <p>This is sandbox content with <strong>HTML</strong> inside.</p>
</div>
{% endsandbox %}
#+end_export

* Quote block

#+begin_quote
Measure twice, cut once. This is a famous woodworking proverb.
#+end_quote

* Source blocks

#+begin_src python
def hello():
    print("world")
#+end_src

#+begin_src javascript
const x = 42;
console.log(x);
#+end_src

* Mixed content

Some regular text with *emphasis* and =inline code= alongside
shortcodes.

{% alert 'This alert is mixed with regular content', 'warning', 'Watch out!' %}

#+begin_quote
Another quote block after an alert.
#+end_quote
